content.label: Identify sqrt(x^2 + a^2) -> x = a tan theta
content.applies_when: the integrand has the form sqrt(x^2 + a^2) with a = 1, which calls for the tangent substitution x = tan theta

content.meaning: let x = tan theta so that dx = sec^2 theta dtheta and sqrt(x^2 + 1) = sec theta

content.label: Secant-cubed reduction result
content.symbolic: integral sec^3 theta dtheta = (1/2)(sec theta tan theta + ln|sec theta + tan theta|) + C

content.concept: tan theta = x, sec theta = sqrt(x^2 + 1)
content.meaning: from x = tan theta the right triangle gives tan theta = x and sec theta = sqrt(x^2 + 1)

content.label: Substitute and simplify
content.action: substitute x = tan theta to get integral sec theta * sec^2 theta dtheta = integral sec^3 theta dtheta
content.purpose: clear the radical and reduce to a secant-cubed integral

content.label: Apply the sec^3 reduction
content.action: apply the standard reduction integral sec^3 theta dtheta = (1/2)(sec theta tan theta + ln|sec theta + tan theta|)

content.label: Convert back from theta to x
content.action: use tan theta = x, sec theta = sqrt(x^2 + 1): (x/2) sqrt(x^2 + 1) + (1/2) ln(x + sqrt(x^2 + 1)) + C
content.purpose: return the antiderivative to the original variable x
